segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Yeah, and they can also be understood on different levels.
segments.zh: 是啊，而且还能从不同层面去理解。这些角色太有深度了。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: The characters have such depth.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Right – which allows directors to experiment and find new angles.
segments.zh: 没错——这让导演们可以尝试并找到新的角度。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: That's really important because …

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Right, everyone, let's make a start.
segments.zh: 好了，各位，我们开始吧。过去几节课，我们一直在探讨为什么一些世界语言在衰落，今天我要介绍另一个影响语言及其使用者的因素，那就是技术，特别是数字技术。为了说明其影响，我将重点介绍冰岛语，这种语言大约有32.1万人使用，其中大部分居住在冰岛——北大西洋中的一个岛屿。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Over the past few sessions, we've been considering the reasons why some world languages are in decline, and today I'm going to introduce another factor that affects languages, and the speakers of those languages, and that's technology and, in particular, digital technology.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In order to illustrate its effect, I'm going to focus on the Icelandic language, which is spoken by around 321,000 people, most of whom live in Iceland – an island in the North Atlantic Ocean.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The problem for this language is not the number of speakers – even though this number is small.
segments.zh: 这种语言的问题不在于使用人数——尽管人数很少。也不在于向英语等其他语言借词。事实上，冰岛语的词汇量在不断增加，因为当使用者需要新词时，他们倾向于自己创造，而不是从其他语言借用。这一切使得冰岛语成为一种非常特殊的语言——在过去的一千年里变化很小，却能处理与计算机和数字技术相关的21世纪概念。例如，网络浏览器这个词……冰岛语是vafri，来自动词“漫游”。我想不出更合适的词了，因为浏览互联网时你的思维正是这样做的。还有冰岛语中播客这个词——太难发音了！等等。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Nor is it about losing words to other languages, such as English.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, the vocabulary of Icelandic is continually increasing because when speakers need a new word for something, they tend to create one, rather than borrowing from another language.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: All this makes Icelandic quite a special language – it's changed very little in the past millennium, yet it can handle twenty-first-century concepts related to the use of computers and digital technology.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Take, for example, the word for web browser … this is vafri in Icelandic, which comes from the verb 'to wander'.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I can't think of a more appropriate term because that's exactly what you do mentally when you browse the internet.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then there's an Icelandic word for podcast – which is too hard to pronounce!

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And so on.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Icelandic, then, is alive and growing, but – and it's a big but – young Icelanders spend a great deal of time in the digital world and this world is predominantly English.
segments.zh: 因此，冰岛语是活着的，并且在发展，但是——这是一个很大的但是——冰岛的年轻人花大量时间在数字世界里，而这个世界主要是英语。想想智能手机。它们直到相对较近才出现，但如今年轻人无时无刻不在用它们读书、看电视或电影、玩游戏、听音乐等等。显然，这在很多方面是好事，因为它促进了他们的双语能力，但英语在虚拟世界中的影响程度是惊人的，而且这一切发生得非常快。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Think about smartphones.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They didn't even exist until comparatively recently, but today young people use them all the time to read books, watch TV or films, play games, listen to music, and so on.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Obviously, this is a good thing in many respects because it promotes their bilingual skills, but the extent of the influence of English in the virtual world is staggering and it's all happening really fast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For their parents and grandparents, the change is less concerning because they already have their native-speaker skills in Icelandic.
segments.zh: 对于他们的父母和祖父母来说，这种变化不那么令人担忧，因为他们已经掌握了冰岛语的母语技能。但对于年轻的使用者来说——嗯，结果有点令人不安。例如，教师发现冰岛中学的操场对话可以完全用英语进行，而教更小孩子的老师报告说，他们的班级用英语描述图片内容比用冰岛语更容易。这一切非常真实且令人担忧的后果是，冰岛的年轻一代正面临失去母语的风险。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But for young speakers – well, the outcome is a little troubling.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, teachers have found that playground conversations in Icelandic secondary schools can be conducted entirely in English, while teachers of much younger children have reported situations where their classes find it easier to say what is in a picture using English, rather than Icelandic.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The very real and worrying consequence of all this is that the young generation in Iceland is at risk of losing its mother tongue.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Of course, this is happening to other European languages too, but while internet companies might be willing to offer, say, French options in their systems, it's much harder for them to justify the expense of doing the same for a language that has a population the size of a French town, such as Nice.
segments.zh: 当然，其他欧洲语言也面临同样的情况，但虽然互联网公司可能愿意在其系统中提供法语选项，但对于一个使用人口只有法国一个城镇（比如尼斯）大小的语言来说，他们很难证明同样的投入是合理的。冰岛语的另一个缺点是语法，它比大多数语言复杂得多。目前，科技巨头根本不愿意解决这个问题。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The other drawback of Icelandic is the grammar, which is significantly more complex than in most languages.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: At the moment, the tech giants are simply not interested in tackling this.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, what is the Icelandic government doing about this?
segments.zh: 那么，冰岛政府对此采取了什么措施呢？大量资金正被投入到一个语言技术基金中，希望这能推动冰岛本土应用及其他社交媒体和数字系统的开发，但显然这将是一场艰难的斗争。

words.def: 艰难的斗争，艰巨的任务

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, large sums of money are being allocated to a language technology fund that it is hoped will lead to the development of Icelandic sourced apps and other social media and digital systems, but clearly this is going to be an uphill struggle.

pairs.note: 题干中的'approximately 31 speakers'是错误数字，实际原文是'around 321,000 people'，表示大约321,000人。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是大约有31个说话者，但原文中说的是'around 321,000 people'，所以通过数字替换定位答案。

items.prompt: has a 32 that is still growing

pairs.p: vocabulary of Icelandic is continually increasing
pairs.note: 题干说'仍在增长的32'，原文说'词汇量持续增加'，32对应vocabulary，growing对应increasing。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'32'是题号，实际问的是'仍在增长的东西'，原文定位句提到'vocabulary of Icelandic is continually increasing'，因此答案就是vocabulary。

items.prompt: has its own words for computer-based concepts, such as web browser and 33

pairs.p: twenty-first-century concepts related to the use of computers and digital technology
pairs.note: 题干用'computer-based concepts'概括，原文用更具体的描述替换。

pairs.p: Take, for example, the word for web browser … this is vafri in Icelandic, which comes from the verb 'to wander'. I can't think of a more appropriate term because that's exactly what you do mentally when you browse the internet. Then there's an Icelandic word for podcast
pairs.note: 题干举例'web browser'和'podcast'，原文先讲web browser例子，再引出podcast。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'Take, for example, the word for web browser'之后，紧接着'Then there's an Icelandic word for podcast'，即可找到答案。

items.prompt: are big users of digital technology, such as 34

pairs.p: spend a great deal of time in the digital world
pairs.note: 题干说'大量使用数字技术'，原文说'花大量时间在数字世界'，意思相同。
题干用'such as'举例，原文用'Think about'引出例子。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到年轻人花大量时间在数字世界，并用'Think about smartphones'引出例子，因此答案就是smartphones。

pairs.note: 题干说'变得非常快'，原文说'发生得非常快'，意思相同，只是换了一种说法。
题干中的35是题号，对应答案bilingual，原文直接出现bilingual skills，定位答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'变得非常快'对应原文'happening really fast'，同时题干中的35对应答案bilingual，原文在提到bilingual skills后说影响发生得很快，因此定位到答案。

items.prompt: are having discussions using only English while they are in the 36 at school

pairs.q: while they are in the 36 at school
pairs.p: playground conversations in Icelandic secondary schools
pairs.note: 原文的'playground'直接对应题干中的空，且'at school'对应'schools'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'playground conversations in Icelandic secondary schools'，其中'playground'就是答案，且'conversations ... in English'对应题干中的'discussions using only English'。

items.prompt: are better able to identify the content of a 37 in English than Icelandic

pairs.note: 题干用'content of a picture'，原文用'what is in a picture'，意思相同，是解释性替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干说'identify the content of a picture'，原文说'say what is in a picture'，两者对应，因此答案就是picture。

items.prompt: write very little in Icelandic because of the small number of speakers and because of how complicated its 38 is

pairs.note: 原文用complex代替题干中的complicated，是同义替换。
题干中的38对应原文的grammar，是数字指代，需根据上下文理解。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到冰岛语的语法比其他语言复杂得多，题干中'complicated its 38'的38指代语法，通过'complex'与'complicated'的同义替换定位。

items.prompt: is worried that young Icelanders may lose their 39 as Icelanders
is worried about the consequences of children not being 40 in either Icelandic or English
